text.title: AI PM Tool Evaluation Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules with rationale + source for ai-pm-tool-evaluation-framework. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Vendor MUST disclose data-processing region for each AI feature and offer EU-only / US-only / customer-owned options in writing.

rationale.source: GDPR Art. 28-29 + EU-US Data Privacy Framework

rule.rationale: GDPR Art. 28-29 + EU-US Data Privacy Framework — undisclosed residency is a procurement blocker for EU customers.

rule.statement: Vendor MUST be scored against a fixture corpus of ≥50 real issues; hallucination rate (fabricated fields, non-existent linked issues, invented ACs) MUST be <5% to pass.
rule.rationale: Anthropic / OpenAI eval methodology — claimed hallucination rates without an in-house fixture are unverifiable; in-house fixture forces ground-truth.

rule.statement: AI features MUST round-trip issue state without lossy edits (status, assignee, links, custom fields preserved bit-for-bit on save).
rule.rationale: Atlassian + Linear product docs — silent state edits are the #1 user-reported AI-feature complaint.

rule.statement: Per-user-month cost MUST be itemised AI-add-on vs base seat; total AI cost MUST be ≤30% of base seat cost or carry written executive approval.
rule.rationale: FinOps Foundation — bundled AI features hide marginal cost; itemisation forces accountability.

rule.statement: Contract MUST grant the buyer right to export AI-generated summaries, threads, and embeddings in machine-readable form on termination.
rule.rationale: Vendor lock-in is the dominant post-purchase regret; explicit export rights are non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Vendor MUST publish prompt-injection mitigations (e.g. structured-output enforcement, allowlisted tool calls) and pass a red-team injection sample on the fixture corpus.
rule.rationale: OWASP LLM Top 10 (LLM01 prompt injection) — untested vendors leak through the issue tracker into Slack / email.

rule.statement: All AI actions MUST be logged with user, timestamp, prompt, response, tool calls — accessible to admins, retained ≥90 days.
rule.rationale: SOC 2 CC7.2 — audit logs missing fail SOC review.

rule.statement: Vendor scored on integration depth against the team's stack (SCM, chat, calendar, observability) — count of native, count of webhook-only, count of unsupported.
rule.rationale: Integration surface predicts adoption; webhook-only often falls back to manual sync.

rule.statement: If procurement scope is a single-dev trial or vendor is enterprise-mandated, return `skipped`.
